new_classes.definition_en: The geographic site encompassing the Italian regions of the designations of origin 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco' and 'Asolo — Prosecco', as defined in the EU legal text for CN subheading 2204 10 15.

A quality borne by a sparkling wine indicating that it was produced exclusively from grapes of the 'Glera' vine variety, as required for the Prosecco protected designation of origin under CN subheading 2204 10 15.

The process of producing Prosecco sparkling wine from Glera grapes harvested within the protected designation of origin regions 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco' and 'Asolo — Prosecco' in Italy.

Sparkling wine with a protected designation of origin (PDO), produced from grapes of the 'Glera' vine variety harvested in the Italian regions of the designations of origin 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco' and 'Asolo — Prosecco', classified under CN subheading 2204 10 15.

coverage_explanation: All principal discriminating criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) sparkling wine character via SparklingWineQuality; (2) protected designation of origin (PDO) geographic constraint via the ProseccoRegionSite (covering all four named appellations: 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', 'Asolo — Prosecco') linked through ProseccoProductionProcess via BFO:occurs_in; (3) exclusive Glera grape variety constraint via GleraGrapeVarietyQuality; (4) disjointness from the sibling Champagne class (22041011). A minor gap (−0.05): the ontology cannot currently enumerate the four individual named appellations as distinct sub-sites, nor formally assert the PDO regulatory status as a quality individual (no PDO quality class yet in TBox), so these are described textually in the definition rather than as formal restrictions.
